xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfRegisteredOfficeOfCom: 1st Floor, August Kranti Bhawan, Bhikaji Cama Place, R.K. Puram, New Delhi-110066
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfCorporateOfficeOfComp: 1st Floor, August Kranti Bhawan, Bhikaji Cama Place, R.K. Puram, New Delhi-110066
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfContactPerson: Mrs. Kalpana Dubey - Company Secretary and Compliance Officer
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheAssessorOrAssurer: Reasonable Assurance for BRSR Core - Annex. I
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescriptionOfBusinessActivity: RVNL is carrying out planning, development, and project management of execution of railway-related infrastructure projects
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProductOrServiceSoldByTheEntit: Planning, development, and project management of execution of railway-related infrastructure projects.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ABriefOnTypesOfCustomersExplan: The Indian Railways, together with its many departments and companies, are the main clientele of RVNL. As a provider of railway infrastructure, RVNL collaborates closely with Indian Railways.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheHoldingOrSubsidiaryAs: Rail Vikas Nigam Limited Company (One Person)(Kingdom of Saudi Arabia)
Shimla Bypass Kaithlighat Shakral Private Limited
Indian Port Rail & Ropeway Corporation Limited
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksStakeHolderGroupFromWho: RVNL does not cater to retail customers
RVNL does not cater to retail customers
The pending complaints were resolved after the closing of the fiscal year.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Projects involve high energy use (machinery, lighting, etc.), Indirect emissions from contractors
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Implement energy-efficient equipment and machinery; Transition to renewable energy sources where feasible (e.g., solar lighting at sites) Adopt green building principles; require contractors to report and manage their emissions Implement Scope 3 emissions tracking and reduction strategies.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Construction uses large quantities of steel, cement, ballast, etc.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: NA
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Water use in curing, dust suppression; tanker dependency
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: NA
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Debris, metal scrap generated at project sites
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: NA
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Projects in ecologically sensitive zones
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Conduct Environmental Impact Assessments (EIA) Avoid protected areas Implement biodiversity management plans Carry out compensatory afforestation.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Construction activities near populated areas
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Use noise barriers Implement wet suppression for dust; Restrict working hours near residential areas Conduct ambient air quality monitoring.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Infrastructure must now be built to withstand extreme climate risks (floods, heat, etc.) across regions
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Integrate climate risk assessments in the project planning stage Adopt climate-resilient design standards Select materials suited for extreme weather conditions Elevate infrastructure in flood-prone areas Install early-warning and drainage systems Cllaborate with climate experts to model regional impacts.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Global sourcing of inputs (rails, steel, signaling) can be impacted by war, trade bans, or sanctions
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Diversify suppliers Maintain buffer inventory Include contractual clauses for delivery assurance Assess suppliers for ESG risks.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Environmental / Social - Environmental & Social Impact Assessments (ESIA)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Required by most foreign governments for infrastructure projects
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Develop project-specific ESIA reports Ensure stakeholder consultations Comply with IFC or World Bank safeguards as required.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Large-scale public procurement and corruption risks
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Enforce transparent tender processes; Apply e-procurement; rotate procurement staff Establish whistleblower and grievance redressal mechanisms.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: As a PSU, RVNL is subject to investor and MoR scrutiny
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: NA
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Governance - Compliance with Environmental & Labour Laws
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Multiple agencies (CEA, CPCB, Labour Dept.) monitor operations
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Set up internal compliance tracking systems Appoint a dedicated compliance officer Conduct regular internal audits; training on legal updates.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Sensitive data on tenders, contracts, project finances
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Implement data encryption and firewall systems Conduct cybersecurity audits Train staff on phishing and data safety protocols.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Operating in multiple countries exposes RVNL to varying legal, tax, labor, and environmental laws
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Engage local legal advisors Establish a compliance checklist by country Monitor policy changes regularly.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Governance - Foreign Exchange Risk / Local Content Rules
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Cost estimation and contracts in international markets must factor in currency volatility and local sourcing laws
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Hedge forex exposure throug Forward contracts Price in contingencies Renegotiate long-term contracts with flexible clauses.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Governance / Environmental - Green Infrastructure Financing / International ESG Benchmarks
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Access to green finance (ADB, World Bank, etc.) requires advanced ESG disclosures and alignment
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: NA
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: High-risk activities in rail electrification, bridge construction
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Enforce strict safety protocols Conduct risk assessments Provide mandatory PPE Adopt ISO 45001 Ensure contractor compliance.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Project disruption, land access issues, social unrest
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Conduct proactive stakeholder engagement and social impact assessments Establish a formal grievance redressal mechanism accessible to local communities Ensure continuous dialogue through local liaison officers Incorporate feedback into project planning to prevent delays and unrest.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Social - Labour Rights in Supply Chain
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Large contractual and migrant workforce
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Enforce supplier code of conduct aligned with national labour laws and ILO standards Ensure proper documentation, wage payments, and working conditions Conduct regular audits of contractors; offer awareness programs on worker rights and safety.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Gender/diversity inclusion in project management roles
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: NA
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Social - Skill Development for Local Communities
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Upskilling locals along project routes
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: NA
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Social - Cultural Sensitivity and Stakeholder Engagement
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: International projects require local language, norms, and community trust
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Engage local stakeholders early in the project lifecycle Hire local liaison officers and translators Conduct cultural sensitivity training for project teams Aadapt project plans to respect local customs and norms Build trust through transparent communication and shared benefits.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Social - Human Rights Due Diligence (incl. migrant labor)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Need to ensure ethical labor practices especially in subcontractor chain across regions
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Conduct human rights impact assessments Require subcontractors to comply with international labor standards (ILO, UNGPs) Monitor working conditions regularly Include human rights clauses in contracts Provide training and grievance mechanisms for migrant labor.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Social - Workforce Diversity and Cross-cultural Management
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Managing teams across different states and nations requires inclusive and conflict-sensitive policies
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: NA

Indian labour codes, ISO 45001, ISO 14001, ISO 9001
Indian labour codes, ISO 45001, ISO 14001, ISO 9001

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:SpecificCommitmentsGoalsAndTar: We have adopted ESG platform provided by Planet Sustech Private Limited and giving training to our team members. Our data management will be much streamlined from next FY.
We have adopted ESG platform provided by Planet Sustech Private Limited and giving training to our team members. Our data management will be much streamlined from next FY.

We have adopted ESG platform provided by Planet Sustech Private Limited and giving training to our team members. Our data management will be much streamlined from next FY.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PerformanceOfTheEntityAgainstT: This FY year 24-25 we have gathered better data from all our concerned sources (PIU) with the help of ESG tool implemented.
This FY year 24-25 we have gathered better data from all our concerned sources (PIU) with the help of ESG tool implemented.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StatementByDirectorResponsible: We are dedicated to incorporate sustainability and responsibility into our business processes as a responsible corporate entity. Ensuring accountability and transparency in the business sector has advanced significantly with SEBI's introduction of the Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report (BRSR). RVNL is committed to follow the values and rules stated in the BRSR framework and we completely support this endeavour. We think that ethical business practices benefit not just our stakeholders but also the environment and society and that sustainable growth is crucial for long-term success. In our BRSR, we have described our endeavours and accomplishments in several ESG areas in depth such as our social contributions, governance procedures and environmental effects. Strong measures have been put in place to reduce our carbon impact, support diversity and inclusion and maintain the highest moral standards. While acknowledging that we can always do better, we are happy about our progress. We will keep pushing for excellence in sustainability and accountability as we proceed coordinating our objectives with the larger global agenda for sustainable development.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfSpecifiedCommitteeOfT: RVNL has a CSR committee at the Board level for overseeing and addressing sustainability-related issues. The Director (Personnel) heads it.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TopicsOrPrinciplesCoveredUnder: Interlocking principles and circuit design, Simplified Finance for Non-Finance Professional for Enhanced Performance, Public Procurement at IIM Visakhapatnam, Training programme on Leadership Journey for Senior Executives of RVNL, Tunnelling course in IRICEN, Technical Seminar of IPWE(I), Conduct Rules in CPSEs, Ethics & Governance, Addressing Disputes in Public Procurement, System & Procedures of organisation, RTI Act Compliance, Design, Fabrication, Launching and Maintenance of Steel Girder Bridges. Training Empowering Women Leaders, CSR Interventions by CPSEs in Heritage & Culture, Tunnelling, AutoCad, BRSR etc.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AntiCorruptionOrAntiBriberyPol: there is an anti-corruption and anti-bribery policy at RVNL. Keeping in mind the BRSR needs, it was functional in FY 2024–25 in accordance with the NGRBC principles. The policy guarantees legal compliance, clarifies what constitutes bribery and corruption and describes due diligence procedures for outside partners. Further, it focuses on reporting, training and the repercussions of violations. Also, the policy is accessible on RVNL's website and it is regularly monitored and evaluated. Keeping in mind RVNL's unshakable commitment to ethics, the Board of Directors are in charge of interpretation and revisions. The company also offers a whistleblower mechanism that enables voice complaints about any incidents of dishonest or fraudulent, behaviour, etc.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheEntityHaveProcesse: Yes, every Director of the Company discloses his/her concern or interest in the Company(s)/ Body Corporates/ Firms/other Association of Individuals and any change therein, annually or upon any change, which includes the shareholding interest. Further, a declaration is also taken annually from the Directors under the Code of Conduct confirming that they will always act in the interest of the Company and ensure that any other business or personal association which they may have, does not involve any conflict of interest with the operations of the Company and the role therein. Also, in the meetings of the Board, the Directors abstain from participating in the items in which they are concerned or interested. For identifying and tracking conflicts of interest involving the Directors/KMPs of the Company, the Corporate Secretarial team maintains a database of the Directors and the entities in which they are interested. This list is shared with the finance department for monitoring and tracking transaction(s) entered by the Company with such parties. Additionally, the Senior Management also affirms annually that they have not entered into any material, financial and commercial transactions, which may have a potential conflict with the interest of the Company at large. The Code of Conduct: https://rvnl.org/RVNL_cms/uploads/copolicy/Code_of_conduct-_RVNL.pdf
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMechanismAvailableToR: Employees at RVNL have a variety of channels for resolving concerns, including direct connection with department heads or HR, the CVC platforms, CPGRAMS and the E-sampark Portal. This inclusive strategy also applies to temporary staff members. Grievances are reported via approved means and assessed by a committee that looks into them. Proposed solutions are shared transparently; ensuring that employees are updated throughout the process. This underscores RVNL's commitment to a harmonious work environment achieved by promptly resolving grievances.

Employees at RVNL have a variety of channels for resolving concerns, including direct connection with department heads or HR, the CVC platforms, CPGRAMS and the E-sampark Portal. This inclusive strategy also applies to temporary staff members. Grievances are reported via approved means and assessed by a committee that looks into them. Proposed solutions are shared transparently; ensuring that employees are updated throughout the process. This underscores RVNL's commitment to a harmonious work environment achieved by promptly resolving grievances.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOccupationalHealthAnd: RVNL has policy on SAFETY, HEALTH AND ENVIRONMENT and same can be accessed on the website of RVNL. The Company prioritizes the employee health through various initiatives like organizing health camps on T.B. , Hepatitis, Dental Checkups, Cancer etc. Link of Policy- https://rvnl.org/RVNL_cms/uploads/copolicy/Safety_Health_and_Environment_Policy.pdf
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DesclosureOfTheProcessesUsedTo: The business adheres to ISO 45001 standards for workplace safety. Hazard identification is based on employee feedback, audits and routine inspections. Thorough risk valuations measure the exposure, severity and efficacy of controls. Thorough documentation keeps track of risks and safeguards. Comprehensive safety is ensured by proactive danger assessments and employee engagement.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProcessesForWorkersToReportThe: RVNL does not have any worker.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMeasuresTakenByTheE: RVNL has implemented a number of safeguards to ensure a healthy and safe work environment. These include putting in accessibility ramps and making it easier for staff members who need mobility aids to move around. In addition, the installation of accessible elevators guarantee vertical mobility, assisting those with restricted mobility between levels. Dedicated restrooms are designed to meet the requirements of people with disabilities. RVNL also adheres to ISO 45001 standards for occupational health and safety. These collective efforts demonstrate RVNL's commitment to create an inclusive and secure work environment for all employees.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: Our steadfast commitment to maintain safety regulations and thorough health and safety evaluations have allowed us to stop these kind of things from happening. By regularly assessing our procedures and working environment, we can quickly resolve any possible risk or issue and provide a safe and responsible work environment for our staff.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMeasuresUndertakenByT: We have internal control mechanism is in place to ensure this activity.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesForIdentif: RVNL. defines its stakeholders as all individuals, groups, organizations, and communities that are directly or indirectly impacted by its operations and value chain. Guided by its commitment to delivering sustained value, the Company places strong emphasis on fostering long-term relationships with its stakeholders. Key stakeholders are identified through a materiality-driven approach, focusing on those who significantly influence or are affected by the Company’s activities. These include customers, investors, regulatory bodies, employees, contract workers, local communities, and various government entities. RVNL actively engages with its stakeholders to understand their expectations and feedback, ensuring that its business practices and processes evolve in response to these insights.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProvideTheProcessesForConsulta: At RVNL, stakeholder engagement mechanism plays a crucial role in strengthening and diversifying relationship with stakeholders. This, in turn, helps to identify key material issues affecting the Company's growth. Further, the stakeholder engagement and materiality assessment conducted in FY2024-25 led to prioritizing the material issues, mapping relevant risks, and developing risk mitigation strategies. The main outcome of the stakeholder engagement exercise was the identification and prioritization of material issues related to environmental, social, governance and economic aspects.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesAsToHowTheIn: RVNL has always maintained a regular and proactive engagement with the Company's key stakeholders, allowing it to effectively work on its ESG strategies and be transparent about the outcomes. In response to current regulations and interactions with stakeholders, the Company performs periodic evaluations to update and revise policies as needed.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesOfEngagement: For more information please refer to CSR Section on our website at https://rvnl.org/csr
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Annual General Meetings, Website, Stock Exchange, Website, Investor Conferences, E-mails, etc.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Business Performance, Corporate Governance, Major work orders, etc.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Employee Portal, Meetings, E-mails, Notice Board, Website, etc.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Information, Events, Trainings, Business Activities, etc.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheInternalMechanismsI: RVNL is committed to upholding human rights and ensuring that all individuals are treated with dignity and respect. We recognize the importance of addressing human rights complaints effectively. To achieve this, we have implemented a comprehensive framework that provides various channels for stakeholders to report their concerns. The CPGRAMS (Centralized Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System) offers a convenient platform for lodging complaints, ensuring transparency and timely resolution. In addition, our Sampark Portal serves as a userfriendly interface that facilitates direct communication and engagement, allowing stakeholders to share their feedback and grievances directly with us. For those who prefer traditional channels, we welcome direct letters and value written correspondence as a means for meaningful dialogue
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsToPreventAdverseCons: To avoid unfavourable outcomes in situation of harassment and discrimination, RVNL employs a number of crucial procedures. First, keeping complainants' identity private to protect them from reprisals and promotes reporting. The impartiality and reliability of the results are ensured by conducting an impartial inquiry by an independent party. Complainants are assisted in navigating the investigative process by providing them with strong support such as counselling and legal aid. RVNL implements remedial measures such as fines, payments or policy improvements, in response to verified claims. These steps guarantee fair handling of complaints. In order to create a work environment where discrimination and harassment are not allowed, RVNL has also implemented a clear anti-harassment policy, provides thorough training and cultivates a respectful culture. Through the use of these processes, RVNL fosters a welcoming and secure atmosphere that encourages impartial grievance investigations and protect complainants from unfavourable outcomes.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheScopeAndCoverageOf: No Human Rights Due Diligence was conducted
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfWasteManagementPracti: RVNL direct work scope does not have any usage of hazardous and toxic chemicals. However for contractors working on RVNL projects are sensitized and advised to take the prescribed precaution while using such chemicals if any.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DisclosureWebLinkOfEntityAtWhi: The Company has a Business Plan in place ensuring future Business Continuity. The plan focuses on future business prospects in Railways and other Infrastructure Sectors like Roads, Metros, Highways, Logistics and Aviation, Solar Projects, Telecom Connectivity etc. Disaster management Plan - All safety equipment, physical tools and backup plans are available on-site to address emergent situations. There is ample equipment, tools and manpower for disaster management.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PublicPolicyAdvocated: The Company through trade bodies and associations put forth several suggestions with respect to the economy in general and the Infrastructure in particular.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsToReceive: We take all concerns about our CSR initiatives seriously and welcome your feedback. Our CSR Department promptly acknowledge and investigate complaints, aiming for swift resolution and implementing preventive measures. We maintain open communication with complainants and value their feedback. We keep comprehensive records for transparency and accountability. Our commitment is to uphold CSR principles and ensure stakeholder satisfaction.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameAndBriefDetailsOfProjectSI: To provide medical equipment Siemens Fluoroscopy Digital Radiography System in Ramakrishna Mission Seva Pratisthan, Kolkata
Promoting healthcare 3D Imaging system for orthopedic surgery to Bharat Ratna Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Memorial Hospital, Mumbai
To provide Sewage Treatment Plant (STP) of 500 KLD to convert sewage water into reusable water at Mathura Railway Station (UP)
To provide hostel facilities for 30 female nursing staff in Ramakrishna Mission Home of Service Charitable Hospital, Varanasi

Construction of first floor for skill development building and second and third floors for hostel building for inmates of skill development programme of underprivileged youths and women at Jaipur, Rajasthan
Renovation and construction of existing Turiyananda Library-cum-multipurpose hall for underprivileged people of Almora District (Uttarakhand)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:CSRProject: Hostel facilities for 240 tribal boys each in Kutul, Irrakbhatti & Kchhapal in the Aspirational District of Narainpur
School Building for 1200 underprivileged girls students in the Aspirational District of Purbi Singhbhum
To provide Livelihood ehancement projects to girls in Shopia, Phulwama & Kulgam District J&K
To provide skill development training facility for tribal & rural youths at Morabadi in the Aspirational District of Ranchi
Healthcare infrastructure facilities at Swastha-Sanyam Kendra, Bijnor (UP)
To provide 4-storied academic-cum-welfare building for women empowrment school building in Ramakrishna Sarada Mission Sister Nivedita Haritage Museum & Knowledge Centre at Bagh Bazar, Kolkata
To provide mobile medical van to Ramakrishna Sarada Mission Siriti, for their Charitable dispensary
Construction of 3 storied kindergarten school building for girls at Ramakrishna Saraada Mission, Shiksha Mandir, Kolkata
Providing pilgrimage block at Sri Kedarnath 'Dham Charitable Trust in the District of Rudraprayag, Uttarakhand for restoration of buildings & sites of istorical importance
Health support in the existing health centre at Guniyari village, District Bilaspur, Chhattisgarh
To provide toilet blocks 5 different locations in Varanasi, Uttar Pradesh
To provide infrascture facilities at Adarsh Junior High School, Suya & Primary School Suya, Chamoli districts of Uttarakhand
Skill development training in nursing assistant course and domenstic electrical solution course for youth (men & women) to generat employment at Devlali, District Nasik Maharashtra
Provide Livelihood enhancement project by promoting beekeeping enterprise in Tehri Garhwal, Uttarakhand
To provide digital smart library facilities for tribal girls & boys students in the Aspirational District of Malkangiri Odisha”
To provide gymnastics & yoga facilities & to promote sports for 700 residential tribal boys of Ramakrishna Mission Ashrama, Hatamuniguda, in the Aspirational District of Rayagada, Odisha
Construction of shelter for destitute cows & bulls at Augustmuni, District Rudraprayag, Uttarakhand
Skill development of Master Trainers & youth on drug abuse prevention at Haridwar in the Aspirational District of Haridwar, Uttarakhand”
To provide toilet block at Netaji Subash Chandra Bose Hostel, Govt. Inter College, Khirsu, Srinagar, District Paudi Gardwal, Uttarakhand”
Construction of 2nd floor at Sarada Block of charitable Hospital of Ramakrishna Mission Sevashrama, Vrindaban, District Mathura (UP)
Focusing on adopting healthy life style for prevention of cancer in UT of Chandigarh & Punjab
Jan Arogyam Community Healthcare program at Kiranj village in the Aspirational District of Nuh, Haryana
Construction of PCC road in village-Murarpur, District Nalanda, Bihar” for Rural development
To Promote sports amongs rural youths in Delhi NCR
For Construction of School building for higher secondary classes at Ramakrishna Mission, Malliankaranai,of Kancheepuram District, Tamil Nadu.
For Construction of a G+2 School building as an extension for tribal boys at Ramakrishna Mission Asharam, Jhargram, of Jhargram District, West Bengal
Development in the selected rural villages of Tehri and Chamoli in Uttarakhand
For Healthcare for transgender, destitute women & underprivileged people in and around Ramakrisna Math, Baghbazar, Kolkata
To provide primary & Sanskrit education in the Aspirational District of Balangir, Odisha.
For Construction of hostel for underprivileged boys of Almorah District, Uttarakhand
Sustainable livelihood program for women at Khora Village, District Ghaziabad (UP)
Setting up of old age home for senior citizen at village Mevali, in the Aspirational District of Fatehpur, U.P
Jan Arogya community healthcare program in the District of Gautam Buddha Nagar
To provide infrastructure facilities for development of Arunodaya Gholeshapur School at Gholeshapur, Behala, Kolata
To provide sewage tretment plant (STP) of 500 KLD to convert sewage watr into re-usable water at Mathura Railway Station (UP)
Conservation of natural resources and maintaing quality of soil & water at Distrcit Rudraprayag, uttarakhand
Preparation of Material for early Childhood care and Education for promotion of Sanskrit at Delhi NCR
For economic empowerment of women towards sustainable and inclusive development in the Aspirational Dist. Of Haridwar/Uttrakhand"
Installation of drinking water vending machines and Eco-friendly public toilets at Badrinath in the District of Chamoli, Utarakhand
Digital skill & literacy training program ie., skill on wheels to underprivileged youth and women in the Amravati & Nagpur Districts of Maharashtra
Renovation of 65 toilet blocks & construction of 15 new toilet blocks at Ramakrishna, Mission Vivek Nagar, Agartala, Tripura
To provide vehicle to Ramakrishna Mission Vivekananda Institute of Values, Gurugram for smooth implantation of value education program
To provide medical equipment & other medical facilities at Healthcare centre, Ganiyari, Bilaspur, Chhattisgarh
Setting up of homes & hostels for women and orphans at Kadungalloor, Aluva, Ernakulam District of Kerala

To provide infrastructure facilities to students of underprivileged background with modern sports & games playgrounds for Bhartheeya Vidyanikethan Society at Trikkur Gopalkrishna Sashthri & Lakshmi Memorial Saraswathy Vidya Nikethan, Trissur, Kerala
For distribution of food to 110 orphans, destitute, senior citizens & bed ridden patients at kurumbapalayam, Coimbatore (TN)
Construction of Patanjali Yoga Training & Research Center at Vill: Muvattupuzhu, District Ernkulam, Kerala
Construction of Shed for 400 stray animals at Goida, District Gajam, Odisha
To provide free drinking water facilitites at the Maha Kumbh Mela, 2025 Prayagraj, U.P through Pi-lo smart water ATM
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsInPlaceTo: As RVNL primarily serves the Indian Railways and does not directly cater to retail customers, it receives complaints through direct communication via mail and letters from the Ministry. Additionally, RVNL utilizes the CPGRAMS portal, which serves as an effective mechanism for receiving complaints. Through this portal, consumers can submit their grievances and feedback, allowing RVNL to promptly address and resolve any issues.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WeblinkWhereInformationOnProdu: The Company’s website provides detailed information on the projects implemented across the country. - Website - https://rvnl.org/home
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StepsTakenToInformAndEducateCo: Various measures are taken during the construction of the projects and maintenance.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsInPlaceToInformConsu: Through E-mail, Text messages, social media, etc.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple9ExplanatoryText: The company provides information about its projects, including various features of the projects.
